content.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
content.summary: Apple is embedding practical AI features across iOS 27 beyond the headlining Siri overhaul, including intelligent bill splitting, automated password updates, one-tap message suggestions, and AI-organized Safari tabs — all running on-device via Apple Intelligence.
content.summary_1: While Apple's revamped Siri grabbed headlines at WWDC 2026, iOS 27 delivers a suite of smaller but highly practical AI features woven into existing apps. Apple Intelligence powers bill splitting via photo of a restaurant receipt, automated password upgrades for compromised credentials, one-tap contextual suggestions in Messages (add to calendar, find photos, set reminders), Call Context that surfaces reservation codes during customer service calls, and natural language Shortcuts creation. Safari gains AI-organized tab groups based on browsing context. The Home app consolidates related smart home events into single notifications. All features operate on-device for privacy, with the public beta arriving soon and general release expected in fall 2026. Apple's strategy emphasizes making software feel smarter rather than pushing users toward explicit chatbot interactions.
content.body_text: iOS 27 ships a broad set of Apple Intelligence features beyond the headlining Siri AI overhaul announced at WWDC 2026. Key additions: (1) Bill Splitting — photograph a receipt, Apple Intelligence extracts items and totals, lets diners split via Apple Cash. (2) Password Update — AI agents autonomously navigate websites to replace weak or breached passwords with strong alternatives. (3) Messages One-Tap Suggestions — context-aware prompts appear in chats: add events to Calendar, send relevant photos, set reminders, all from a single tap. (4) Call Context — during a phone call with a business, iOS surfaces confirmation codes from Mail on the lock screen, running entirely on-device. (5) Calendar Natural Language — describe an event in plain text and Calendar creates it, extracting contacts, locations and title. (6) Shortcuts Vibe Coding — describe what you want your iPhone to do; iOS builds the automation for you. (7) Home App consolidation — multiple sequential smart home events grouped into single meaningful notifications. (8) Safari Tab Organizer — AI groups open tabs by inferred topic into named tab groups. Developer beta is live; public beta and fall release to follow.

tech_insights.impact_summary: Apple Intelligence in iOS 27 demonstrates on-device agentic AI becoming mainstream — password managers, calendar, messaging and home automation all gain autonomous AI behaviors without cloud dependency.
actions_developer: Test your iOS apps against the iOS 27 developer beta now — Apple Intelligence may surface or interact with your app's data (emails, calendar events, contacts) in new ways.
Explore SiriKit and App Intents APIs to expose your app's functionality to Apple's new one-tap suggestion and Shortcuts natural language features.
actions_business: Prepare customer communications explaining new iOS 27 AI features that may affect how users interact with your service (e.g., automated password resets, call context).
Audit your app's notification design — the Home App consolidation model suggests Apple may extend smart grouping to third-party apps in future releases.
